item.drivebysable.cable_cutter.tooltip.s: Bad connection? Too many cables? Just cut them all!
Saves same-sublevel cable links into schematics and restores them when the sublevel comes back
block.drivebysable.cable_hub.tooltip.sum: Bind your controller to this block, and control this network from far away!
block.drivebysable.advanced_cable_hub.to: Bind your controller to this block, and control this network from far away!
drivebysable.ponder.wires.text_1: Disclaimer: Cable networks only work on assembled Sable sublevels!
drivebysable.ponder.wires.text_2: Connect a redstone source to a block,
drivebysable.ponder.wires.text_3: And the redstone power is transmitted over cable
drivebysable.ponder.wires.text_4: Connect a block to a block,
drivebysable.ponder.wires.text_5: And adjacent redstone power is transmitted over cable
drivebysable.ponder.lectern.text_1: Connections from lectern controllers have a special feature,
drivebysable.ponder.lectern.text_2: they have multiple channels to transmit over, corresponding to the controller keys.
drivebysable.ponder.lectern.text_3: Select the channel you want using the scrollwheel when holding Cables.
drivebysable.ponder.save.text_1: What if you wanted to save your network to schematic, and reload it?
drivebysable.ponder.save.text_2: Place a Backup Block on your sublevel before you schematic it,
drivebysable.ponder.save.text_3: And internal same-sublevel connections will be restored when the placed schematic is assembled.
drivebysable.ponder.link.text_1: Cross-sublevel schematic relinking is not supported yet.
drivebysable.ponder.link.text_2: Backup Blocks currently preserve only internal same-sublevel cable links.
drivebysable.ponder.link.text_3: Connections that cross into the world or another sublevel are skipped for now.
drivebysable.ponder.link.text_4: Those links can come back later when the blueprint API grows the mapping hooks we need.
drivebysable.configuration.shouldConsume: Whether or not wires should be consumed upon use when in survival mode.
